findings.title: Forced ETH donations can permanently brick `executeOnOpportunity`

findings.title: Anyone can permissionlessly trigger the hardcoded exploit once the contract is funded

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can front-run the exploit and consume the one-shot opportunity

rejected_candidates.title: Pre-existing WETH can spoof the profit check and make failed runs look successful
rejected_candidates.reason: The code does count pre-existing WETH as part of the post-call ETH balance, but the practical effect is only a misleading success signal and conversion of voluntarily donated WETH into equally trapped ETH. It does not create new extractable value or a realistic protocol-level exploit beyond the already-reported trapped-funds design flaws.
